verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically resident on the matrix face of the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, ectopic surface expression of F1-ATP synthase components (including beta subunit) has been documented on the plasma membrane of hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and multiple tumor types, where it acts as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and coupling factor 6. Surface ATP synthase has been validated by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact cells, and is an active oncology target (e.g., citrate-based inhibitors, antibodies against ecto-F1). Tumor/tissue-restricted ecto-display alongside dominant mitochondrial localization fits cell_state_induced. Not classical_surface_receptor — no TM domain of its own.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether surface ecto-ATP synthase display in tumors/hepatocytes is better classified as tissue_restricted_surface vs cell_state_induced; mechanism of PM trafficking remains incompletely defined.
